question: Is the image composed as a strict top-down flat lay (90-degree angle)?
Are exactly eight distinct stringed instruments visible in the image?
Are the instruments arranged in a radial pattern, evenly spaced around the center?
Is the background surface a light-colored circular white ash wood board (visible wood grain)?
Is the Japanese shamisen depicted with a rectangular/box-shaped body (skin-covered appearance) and three strings?
Is the Indian sitar depicted with a large gourd resonator, long neck with curved metal frets, and multiple strings?
Is the West African kora depicted with a large calabash gourd body and strings arranged in two parallel rows?
Is the Iranian tar depicted with its characteristic double-bowl (figure-8) shaped body and membrane covering?
Is the Chinese pipa depicted with a pear-shaped wooden body, four strings, and frets extending onto the soundboard?
Is the Arabic oud depicted with a deep pear-shaped body, short fretless neck, and bent-back pegbox?
Is the Eastern European balalaika depicted with a distinct triangular body shape and three strings?
Are small pieces of textile placed specifically in the gaps between adjacent instruments?
Is a Chinese-style textile (e.g., silk) correctly paired/placed adjacent to the Chinese pipa?
Is an African-style textile (e.g., kente cloth with geometric patterns) correctly paired/placed adjacent to the West African kora?
Is a Persian-style textile (e.g., carpet fragment) correctly paired/placed adjacent to the Iranian tar?
Is the image free of any visible text, labels, or watermarks?
Are all eight instruments fully visible and not significantly cropped at the edges?
